subject: [COMMON] Add a spellchecker tox profile
commit_message:
[COMMON] Add a spellchecker tox profile

inspired from openStack
https://github.com/openstack/qa-specs/blob/master/tox.ini

https://doughellmann.com/blog/tag/sphinxcontrib-spelling/
https://pypi.org/project/sphinxcontrib-spelling/

Issue-ID: OOM-2648
Signed-off-by: Guillaume Lambert <guillaume.lambert@orange.com>
Change-Id: Ia341f96a906c82f88257a885fd66005450a15d16

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-commit-lint/312/ : FAILURE

The Commit title must follow this pattern "[WHAT] A title"

WHAT can be a component name (AAI for example), DOC for documentation, COMMON for common, ...

The title must start with a capital letter (A in the example) and a space must be set between the end of the topic and the actual title: "] A title" ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-commit-lint/312/ )

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

I agree with tox.ini, but we should avoid to have a separate spelling_wordlist.txtfor each project
There is one such file in doc repo:
https://git.onap.org/doc/plain/docs/validwords.txt

Patch Set 5:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 7 on the queue.

Patch Set 5:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 8 on the queue.

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1
> 
> I agree with tox.ini, but we should avoid to have a separate spelling_wordlist.txtfor each project
> There is one such file in doc repo:
> https://git.onap.org/doc/plain/docs/validwords.txt

Let's do this if this is what you prefer.
As a consequence, I've pushed a few updates along in the docs project.

Note that there are pros and cons to do that:
- in particular connectivity and sync issues induced
- but also  the impossiblity to have the new whitelist merged together with the documentation updates here  => this prevents the profile from being votable in jenkins
